title: GharKharcha – Smart Home Expense Tracker

requirements.text: Create the backend actor in backend/main.mo with stable storage for expenses and settings. Expose CRUD functions: addExpense, updateExpense, deleteExpense, getExpenses, getSettings, updateSettings. Expense record fields: id (Text), amount (Float), category (Text), date (Text), paymentMode (Text), notes (Text), createdAt (Int). Settings record fields: monthlyBudget (Float), annualIncome (Float), darkMode (Bool), pinEnabled (Bool), pin (Text).

quotes: expenses: [{ id, amount, category, date, paymentMode, notes, createdAt }]
settings: { monthlyBudget, annualIncome, darkMode, pinEnabled, pin }

acceptanceCriteria: addExpense persists a new expense and returns its id
updateExpense modifies an existing expense by id
deleteExpense removes an expense by id
getExpenses returns all stored expenses
getSettings returns the current settings object
updateSettings persists updated settings fields

requirements.text: Pre-populate the backend with 3 months of realistic sample data for an Indian middle-class family covering January, February, and March 2026. Include all categories (Grocery, Rent, Electricity, Water, Gas, EMI, School Fees, Medical, Travel, Shopping, Other) with monthly totals ranging ₹18,000–₹28,000. This seed data should be inserted once on first deploy if no expenses exist.

quotes: Pre-populate with 3 months of realistic sample data for an Indian middle-class family

acceptanceCriteria: On first load with empty storage, expenses for Jan–Mar 2026 are present
All 11 categories appear in the sample data
Monthly totals fall within the ₹18,000–₹28,000 range
Seed is not re-inserted on subsequent canister restarts

requirements.text: Implement a mobile-first React SPA (max-width 430px, centered on desktop) using the specified color scheme: Primary #0F172A, Accent #22C55E, Background #F1F5F9, Danger #EF4444, Card Background #FFFFFF, Text Primary #1E293B, Text Secondary #64748B. Apply a premium, polished Android-app aesthetic with rounded cards (border-radius 16px), smooth tab-switch animations, and consistent typography throughout.

quotes: The app should look premium and polished

acceptanceCriteria: App container is max 430px wide and centered on desktop screens
All specified hex colors are applied correctly across components
Cards have 16px border radius
Tab switching has a visible smooth transition animation
Layout is fully usable on a 375px-wide mobile viewport

requirements.text: Build a bottom navigation bar with 5 tabs: Home (Dashboard), Calendar, Analysis, Reports, Settings. The active tab should be visually highlighted with the accent green color. Navigation must switch views without page reload.

quotes: App Structure – Bottom Navigation with 5 tabs: Home, Calendar, Analysis, Reports, Settings

acceptanceCriteria: All 5 tabs are visible in the bottom nav bar
Active tab icon and label are highlighted in #22C55E
Tapping each tab renders the corresponding screen
No page reload occurs when switching tabs

requirements.text: Build the Dashboard (Home) tab with: a header showing 'GharKharcha' logo and 'Namaste! 🙏' greeting; a prominent card showing today's total expense; a card showing this month's total expense; a budget remaining card (green text if under budget, red if over); a Recharts PieChart showing category-wise spending for the current month with legend; a recent transactions list (last 5 entries) where each row shows category emoji icon, name, amount in ₹ formatted Indian style, and date in DD MMM YYYY format; and a Smart Insights section with auto-generated insight cards.

quotes: Pie chart: category-wise spending this month

acceptanceCriteria: Today's expense total updates when new expenses are added for today
Month total and budget remaining cards reflect real data
Budget remaining card text is green when under budget and red when over
Pie chart renders with real category-wise data using Recharts
Last 5 transactions are listed with correct category icon, name, ₹ amount, and DD MMM YYYY date
Smart Insights cards are dynamically generated from actual expense data

requirements.text: Build a floating action button (FAB) on the Dashboard (bottom-right, green #22C55E, circular with + icon) that opens an Add Expense bottom sheet/modal. The modal must include: a large ₹ amount input; a category dropdown with emoji icons (🛒 Grocery, 🏠 Rent, ⚡ Electricity, 💧 Water, 🔥 Gas, 💳 EMI, 🏫 School Fees, 🏥 Medical, 🚗 Travel, 🛍️ Shopping, 📦 Other); a date picker defaulting to today; payment mode pill buttons (Cash, UPI, Card, Bank); an optional notes field; and a full-width green Save button. The same modal must support Edit mode when editing an existing expense, pre-filling all fields.

quotes: Quick '+ Add Expense' floating action button (green, bottom right)
Screen 2: Add Expense (Modal/Sheet triggered by FAB)
Also support Edit mode when editing existing expense

acceptanceCriteria: FAB is visible on the Dashboard screen, fixed bottom-right, green and circular
Tapping FAB opens the Add Expense modal/bottom sheet
All 11 categories appear in the dropdown with their emoji icons
Date picker defaults to today's date
Payment mode pill buttons are mutually exclusive and visually indicate selection
Saving a new expense closes the modal, shows a toast notification, and updates all data views
Edit mode pre-fills all fields with the selected expense's existing values
Editing and saving updates the expense and reflects changes immediately

requirements.text: Build the Calendar tab with a monthly grid calendar. Each date cell displays the date number and, below it, the total expense for that day in small text (highlighted if non-zero). Clicking any date opens a bottom sheet/panel showing: the date heading in DD MMM YYYY format, total for that day, and a list of all expenses for that day. Each expense item in the list shows category emoji, name, payment mode, ₹ amount, and Edit/Delete action buttons. The calendar must have prev/next month navigation arrows.

quotes: Each date cell shows total expense for that day
Click any date → show a bottom sheet/panel
Each item: category icon + name + payment mode + amount + edit/delete buttons

acceptanceCriteria: Calendar renders a full monthly grid for the current month
Date cells with expenses show the daily total amount below the date number
Dates with expenses are visually highlighted
Clicking a date opens a bottom sheet with that day's expense list
Each expense row in the day sheet has category emoji, name, payment mode, amount, and edit/delete buttons
Edit button opens the Add/Edit modal pre-filled with that expense
Delete button shows a confirmation dialog before deleting
Prev/next arrows navigate to adjacent months

requirements.text: Build the Analysis tab with two sub-tabs: Monthly and Yearly. Monthly sub-tab: month/year selector with prev/next arrows; big total monthly expense number; a budget progress bar (spent vs budget); percentage comparison with previous month with up/down arrow indicator; a Recharts PieChart for category-wise distribution; a Recharts BarChart for daily spending trend across the month; top spending category highlight; budget status badge (green = under, red = over). Yearly sub-tab: year selector; Recharts BarChart for month-wise totals (Jan–Dec); total yearly expense; average monthly expense; highest spending month highlighted; savings estimate (annual income minus yearly expense).

quotes: Bar chart: daily spending trend for the month
Savings estimation (annual income from settings – yearly expense)

acceptanceCriteria: Monthly/Yearly sub-tabs switch correctly
Month selector changes the displayed month's data
Budget progress bar reflects spent vs monthly budget
Month-over-month comparison shows correct % change with directional arrow
Pie chart renders real category data for selected month
Daily bar chart renders each day of the selected month
Year selector changes the displayed year's data
Monthly bar chart renders 12 bars (Jan–Dec) for the selected year
Highest spending month is visually highlighted
Savings estimate = annualIncome − yearly total expense

requirements.text: Build the Reports tab with a filter panel containing: a from–to date range picker; a category multi-select (all 11 categories); a payment mode filter (Cash, UPI, Card, Bank, All). Below the filters, render a filtered results list showing matching expenses with category icon, name, date, payment mode, and amount. Show a summary bar with total filtered amount and count of transactions. Provide three export buttons: PDF (triggers browser print of the filtered list), CSV/Excel (downloads a .csv file), and Share (copies a text summary to clipboard).

quotes: Export buttons: PDF (print), Excel/CSV (download), Share (copy text)

acceptanceCriteria: Date range filter correctly limits results to the selected range
Category multi-select filters expenses to only selected categories
Payment mode filter works independently and in combination with other filters
Filtered list updates immediately when filters change
Summary shows correct total amount and transaction count for current filter
PDF button triggers browser print dialog
CSV button downloads a valid .csv file with expense data
Share button copies a readable text summary to the clipboard and shows a toast

requirements.text: Build the Smart Insights section (rendered on the Dashboard as a scrollable card section). Insights must be dynamically calculated and include: per-category spend this month in Hindi-style English text (e.g., 'Aapne is mahine Grocery par ₹X kharch kiya'); category-level comparison with last month showing % increase/decrease; a budget alert card when 80%+ of monthly budget is consumed; saving tip suggestions; and a highest-spending category alert card. Each insight is displayed as a distinct card with a colored icon.

quotes: Aapne is mahine Grocery par ₹X kharch kiya
Shopping par 18% zyada kharch kiya pichle mahine se
Budget alert: 'Budget ka 80% use ho gaya!'
Show as cards with colored icons

acceptanceCriteria: Insight cards are rendered on the Dashboard below recent transactions
Category spend insights show real calculated amounts
Month-over-month category comparisons display correct % change
Budget alert card appears only when ≥80% of monthly budget is spent
Highest spending category card shows the correct category name and amount
All insight values update when expense data changes

requirements.text: Build the Settings tab with the following functional sections: Monthly Budget input (₹, save updates backend); Annual Income input (₹, save updates backend); Currency display showing ₹ INR (read-only); Dark Mode toggle that applies a dark theme across the entire app; PIN Lock toggle that, when enabled, prompts the user to set a 4-digit PIN and requires that PIN on app launch; Clear All Data button that shows a confirmation dialog before deleting all expenses; About section showing app name 'GharKharcha' and version '1.0.0'.

quotes: App lock toggle (PIN – simulate with simple PIN entry)
Data management: Clear all data button (with confirmation)

acceptanceCriteria: Saving monthly budget updates the value used in Analysis and Dashboard budget cards
Saving annual income updates the savings estimate in Yearly Analysis
Dark mode toggle switches the app color theme to dark variant and persists across sessions
PIN lock toggle prompts for PIN setup when enabled
On app launch with PIN enabled, a PIN entry screen blocks access until correct PIN is entered
Clear All Data shows a confirmation dialog and, on confirm, removes all expenses
About section displays app name and version 1.0.0

requirements.text: Implement global UX polish across the app: toast notifications for save, update, and delete actions; confirmation dialogs before any destructive action (delete expense, clear all data); empty state messages with descriptive text when no data exists for a view; Indian Rupee formatting (₹1,23,456 style) for all monetary values; DD MMM YYYY date format throughout; and loading states while data is being fetched from the backend.

quotes: All amounts in Indian Rupee format (₹1,23,456)
Dates in Indian format (DD MMM YYYY)

acceptanceCriteria: A toast appears after saving, editing, or deleting any expense
A confirmation dialog appears before delete and clear-all actions
Empty state text is shown when a list or chart has no data
All currency values use Indian number formatting (e.g., ₹1,23,456)
All dates are displayed as DD MMM YYYY (e.g., 02 Mar 2026)
A loading indicator is shown while backend calls are in progress

constraints: All monetary values must use Indian Rupee formatting: ₹1,23,456 (lakh/crore grouping)
All dates must be displayed in DD MMM YYYY format
Recharts must be used for all charts (PieChart and BarChart)
App container max-width must be 430px, centered on desktop
Color palette must strictly follow: Primary #0F172A, Accent #22C55E, Background #F1F5F9, Danger #EF4444, Card #FFFFFF, Text #1E293B / #64748B
Do not edit any files listed in frontend.immutablePaths
All 11 expense categories must be supported throughout the app
nonGoals: Cloud sync or external database integration
Bank account or UPI app integration

required: App icon logo for GharKharcha: a stylized house outline with a rupee symbol inside, on a dark blue (#0F172A) rounded square background with green accent (#22C55E) details (gharkharcha-logo.dim_128x128.png)
